steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what Zhao et al.'s findings best support. Their key result: no significant variation in U-238 concentration or U-234/U-238 AR across multiple depths and locations within the same lake. The passage also establishes that non-uniform values can compromise sampling reliability.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'ought to evaluate how evenly U abundance and AR are distributed': This describes a general recommendation the passage background implies, not what Zhao et al.'s specific finding supports. Their result is about uniformity within their two lakes, not a prescription for all scientists. ✗
  • Choice B — 'challenge the assumption that U abundance and AR sometimes vary from lake to lake': Zhao et al. studied variation within each lake, not between lakes. This misreads the scope of their finding. ✗
  • Choice C — 'researchers can likely rely on localized sampling within each lake': If values are consistent across all depths and locations within a lake, then any single sample is representative of the whole lake — localized sampling is reliable. This follows directly from their result. ✓
  • Choice D — 'the number of samples is sufficient to yield equally reliable information': The passage says nothing about sample count or whether information about sediment transport vs. groundwater quality is equally reliable. This goes beyond the data. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
